page: Google Cloud Platform n1-highmem-16 is a memory-allocated virtual machine instance within the n1 family. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 8 physical Intel Xeon cores utilizing the x86_64 architecture with hyper-threading enabled. The processor operates at a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz and is equipped with a 45 MB L3 cache. The instance is configured with 104 GB of system memory, providing 6.5 GB of RAM per core. It does not include bundled local storage or integrated GPU hardware, and provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core processing is a notable bottleneck, with stress-ng and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the weak bottom 25% category, and single-threaded compression tasks like gzip and bzip3 ranking in the poor bottom 10%. However, multi-core execution scales to average middle-50% performance levels across Geekbench, Passmark, and stress-ng multi-core tests. Memory bandwidth is stable, registering at 81.5 GB/sec for large-block read operations and 43.43 GB/sec for large-block write operations.

This hardware profile offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive workloads that do not require high single-threaded CPU performance. The high memory-to-vCPU ratio supports memory-bound tasks such as database operations, which achieved average scores in Passmark database benchmarks, and Redis caching, which reached 10.99 million operations per second. It is also suitable for static web serving and moderate multi-threaded software compilation, though it is poorly suited for intensive single-threaded computations or large-scale LLM inference.
description: Google Cloud Platform n1-highmem-16 is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs and 104 GB of RAM, powered by a 2.3 GHz Intel Xeon processor. It lacks bundled local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmarks reveal a performance tradeoff: single-core CPU execution and single-threaded compression tasks perform poorly, ranking in the bottom 10% to 25%, while multi-core operations, memory bandwidth, and database performance consistently reach average middle-50% thresholds. This resource distribution makes the instance qualitatively cost-effective for memory-bound applications that benefit from a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio without requiring high single-threaded compute power. It is best suited for database operations, Redis caching, static web serving, and moderate multi-threaded workloads.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n1-highmem-16 is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring 16 vCPUs and 104 GB of RAM. It offers average multi-core and memory performance alongside weak single-core processing.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n1-highmem-16 is an x86_64 VM with 16 vCPUs and 104 GB RAM, offering average multi-core and weak single-core performance.
tagline: A memory-allocated virtual server delivering balanced multi-core performance and high memory capacity for database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated vCPUs and 104 GB of system RAM
Intel Xeon x86_64 processor running at 2.3 GHz
Average multi-core performance and memory bandwidth
Weak single-core and single-threaded compression performance
No bundled local storage or GPU acceleration
Suited for database operations, Redis caching, and web serving
